overview:
## Return Value

A result code. See [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/security-framework-result-codes].

## Discussion

This is the first function in a sequence of encoder functions that you call to sign or encrypt a message. The other functions in the sequence require you to pass in the CMSEncoder reference returned by this function. In many cases, you can call the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/CMSEncode] function alone instead of this sequence of encoder functions.

# CMSEncoderCreate(_:)

**Function**

Creates a CMSEncoder reference.

## Declaration

```swift
func CMSEncoderCreate(_ cmsEncoderOut: UnsafeMutablePointer<CMSEncoder?>) -> OSStatus
```

## Parameters

- **cmsEncoderOut**: On return, points to a CMSEncoder reference. You must use the `CFRelease` function to free this reference when you are finished using it.

## Return Value

A result code. See [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/security-framework-result-codes].

## Discussion

This is the first function in a sequence of encoder functions that you call to sign or encrypt a message. The other functions in the sequence require you to pass in the CMSEncoder reference returned by this function. In many cases, you can call the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/CMSEncode] function alone instead of this sequence of encoder functions.
title: CMSEncoderCreate(_:)
